content:
Is this the only such email you have? In other words, what to other messages with a JPG attachment look like? What about other image formats (PNG, GIF, BMP, etc.)?

If it's just that one message, I would consider it to be somehow corrupt and move on. You might be able to Save the attachments to your local drive and view them outside of Thunderbird with Picture Manager.

If all messages with images, try going to menu item <Settings | General | Files & Attachments> and change the Action for JPG to use some image viewer OTHER THAN Picture Manager. Then re-start Thunderbird, check and see of the message in question uses that other viewer properly, then go back and change the Action back to your preferred Picture Manager.

Is it better?
creator: rednoise

content:
I tried another email with a GIF attachment, and there was no problem. Apparently it is you say, a corrupted set of attached jpeg images.

creator: jyjcraig

content:
The answer is "Yes" unless they do something drastically wrong. If you want to be extra careful, copy all messages to Local Folders, and backup your Profile for good measure. After the account is back on line and you're satisfied that everything is intact, you can delete the Local copies.

It's a good idea to make regular backups of your Thunderbird Profile,
creator: rednoise

content: If it's a network (CalDAV or other, such as Google Calendar), you can create a new calendar attached to it and all your events will be there. If it was a local calendar on the old computer, you can import it from your old Profile. Please describe your situation in more detail and we can give you some advice on how to do it.
creator: rednoise

content: Well, there's no single reply key - you have hit at least two, Ctrl+r, Ctrl+Shift+r, etc. If you haven't done that, your typed keystrokes go nowhere.
creator: rednoise

content: I should have been more clear. I am sometimes in such a hurry to reply that I just start typing with the message still sitting in the inbox. I often start by doing Shift to start with a capital letter, then after a few strokes realize my mistake, only to find that the message had disappeared from my inbox. Where did it go? And how can I get it back. Today, I was able to find it in a thread on my iPad, but that is not always the case. Thanks,
creator: nancy63

content:
Unless you have the Reply window visible and having the focus and accepting your typing, your message is likely to be nowhere. It would depend on what specific keys you hit. You might accidentally make Thunderbird do something sensible, like save a draft (Ctrl+S), but you're more probably going to trigger Thunderbird to do something you don't intend.

Check out the standard keyboard shortcuts for Thunderbird and you might see some of the possible actions:

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/keyboard-shortcuts-thunderbird
creator: rednoise
